title: Cantinoa plectranthoides
intro: Cantinoa plectranthoides, also known as a variety of the herb Cantinoa, is a flowering plant in the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and aromatic leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Cantinoa plectranthoides include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cantinoa plectranthoides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into herbal supplements and topical creams, where its aromatic and therapeutic benefits can be fully utilized.
taxonomy: Cantinoa plectranthoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a member of the genus Cantinoa, which was previously classified under the genus Hyptis. The genus Cantinoa is closely related to the genus Rosmarinus, with both being part of the tribe Mentheae. The tribe Mentheae is further classified under the subfamily Nepetoideae. Further classification includes the family Lamiaceae being part of the order Lamiales.
